  fi
}

stop_agent_db() {
  local IDENTIFIER="$1"
  local CONTAINER="agent-db-${IDENTIFIER}"
  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}

cleanup_agent_dbs() {
  # Remove stopped containers with claude-agent label
  local STOPPED
  STOPPED=$(docker ps -a --filter "label=claude-agent=true" --filter "status=exited" --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$STOPPED" ]; then
    echo "$STOPPED" | while read -r name; do
      docker rm -f "$name" >/dev/null 2>&1
      log "Cleaned up stopped DB container: $name"
    done
  fi
}

# --- Fetch issues labeled "agent" for a team ---

fetch_agent_issues() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local TEAM_NAME="$2"
  local LIMIT="$3"

  local QUERY
  if [ "$SEQUENCED" = true ]; then
    # Include relations to check for blockers
    QUERY="{
      issues(
        filter: {
          team: { name: { eq: \"$TEAM_NAME\" } }
          labels: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }
          state: { type: { nin: [\"completed\", \"canceled\"] } }
        }
        first: $LIMIT
      ) {
        nodes {
          id
          identifier
          title
          description
          team { id name }
          parent { id }
          labels { nodes { id name } }
          relations {
            nodes {
              type
              relatedIssue {
                id
                identifier
                state { type }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }"
  else
    QUERY="{
      issues(
        filter: {
          team: { name: { eq: \"$TEAM_NAME\" } }
          labels: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }
          state: { type: { nin: [\"completed\", \"canceled\"] } }
        }
        first: $LIMIT
      ) {
        nodes {
          id
          identifier
          title
          description
          team { id name }
          parent { id }
          labels { nodes { id name } }
        }
      }
    }"
  fi

  local RESULT
  RESULT=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "$QUERY" | jq '.data.issues.nodes // []')

  if [ "$SEQUENCED" = true ]; then
    # Filter out issues that have incomplete blockers
    echo "$RESULT" | jq '[.[] | select(
      ([.relations.nodes[]? | select(.type == "isBlockedBy") | .relatedIssue.state.type] |
       map(select(. != "completed" and . != "canceled")) | length) == 0
    )]'
  else
    echo "$RESULT"
  fi
}

# --- Worktree management ---

cleanup_worktrees() {
  local REPO_DIR="$1"
  local REPO_NAME
  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
  cd "$REPO_DIR" || return 1

  local WORKTREE_BASE="$HOME/.claude/worktrees/${REPO_NAME}"
  local CLEANED=0
  if [ -d "$WORKTREE_BASE" ]; then
    for wt in "$WORKTREE_BASE"/issue-*; do
      [ -d "$wt" ] || continue
      local ISSUE_ID
      ISSUE_ID=$(basename "$wt" | sed 's/issue-//')

      local PR_STATE
      PR_STATE=$(gh pr list --head "agent/${ISSUE_ID}" --state all --json state --jq '.[0].state // "none"' 2>/dev/null)

      if [ "$PR_STATE" = "MERGED" ] || [ "$PR_STATE" = "CLOSED" ]; then
        log "[$REPO_NAME] Cleaning up worktree for ${ISSUE_ID} (PR ${PR_STATE})"

        # Stop DB container if running
        stop_agent_db "$ISSUE_ID"

        git worktree remove "$wt" --force 2>/dev/null || true
        git branch -D "agent/${ISSUE_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true
        CLEANED=$((CLEANED + 1))
      fi
    done
  fi

  git worktree prune 2>/dev/null || true
  cleanup_agent_dbs
  [ "$CLEANED" -gt 0 ] && log "[$REPO_NAME] Cleaned $CLEANED worktrees"
}

show_status() {
  echo "=== Claude Agent Runner Status ==="
  echo ""

  local WORKSPACES
  WORKSPACES=$(jq -r '.workspaces | keys[]' "$CONFIG")

  while IFS= read -r WS; do
    echo "Workspace: $WS"

    local API_KEY
    API_KEY=$(resolve_api_key "$WS" 2>/dev/null) || { echo "  No API key configured"; echo ""; continue; }

    local TEAMS
    TEAMS=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WS\"].teams | to_entries[] | \"\(.key)|\(.value.repo)|\(.value.local_db // \"none\")\"" "$CONFIG")

    while IFS='|' read -r TEAM_NAME REPO_DIR LOCAL_DB; do
      local REPO_NAME
      REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
      echo "  Team: $TEAM_NAME → $REPO_NAME (db: ${LOCAL_DB})"

      local ACTIVE=0
      local WT_BASE="$HOME/.claude/worktrees/$(basename "$REPO_DIR")"
      if [ -d "$WT_BASE" ]; then
        for wt in "$WT_BASE"/issue-*; do
          [ -d "$wt" ] || continue
          local ID
          ID=$(basename "$wt" | sed 's/issue-//')
          echo "    Active: $ID"
          ACTIVE=$((ACTIVE + 1))
        done
      fi
      [ "$ACTIVE" -eq 0 ] && echo "    No active agents"

      local PENDING
      PENDING=$(fetch_agent_issues "$API_KEY" "$TEAM_NAME" 10 | jq length)
      echo "    Pending: $PENDING issues labeled '$LABEL'"
    done <<< "$TEAMS"
    echo ""
  done <<< "$WORKSPACES"
}

# --- Auto-review agent (Feature 2) ---

auto_review_pr() {
  local REPO_DIR="$1"
  local BRANCH="$2"
  local IDENTIFIER="$3"

  cd "$REPO_DIR" || return 1

  local PR_NUMBER
  PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json number --jq '.number' 2>/dev/null) || return 1
  [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] && return 1

  log "[review] Reviewing PR #${PR_NUMBER} for ${IDENTIFIER}"

  # Get the full diff
  local DIFF
  DIFF=$(gh pr diff "$BRANCH" 2>/dev/null)
  [ -z "$DIFF" ] && { log "[review] No diff found for PR #${PR_NUMBER}"; return 1; }

  # Get PR info
  local PR_TITLE PR_BODY
  PR_TITLE=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json title --jq '.title' 2>/dev/null)
  PR_BODY=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json body --jq '.body' 2>/dev/null)

  # Create a temp file for the review prompt
  local REVIEW_PROMPT
  REVIEW_PROMPT=$(mktemp)
  cat > "$REVIEW_PROMPT" << REVIEW_EOF
You are a senior code reviewer. Review this PR thoroughly.

## PR: ${PR_TITLE}

### Description
${PR_BODY}

### Diff
\`\`\`diff
${DIFF}
\`\`\`

## Your task
Review the code and output ONLY a JSON object with this exact structure:
{
  "verdict": "approve" | "request_changes" | "comment",
  "summary": "1-2 sentence overall assessment",
  "blocking": ["list of issues that MUST be fixed before merge"],
  "important": ["list of issues that SHOULD be fixed"],
  "nits": ["minor style/preference suggestions"],
  "praise": ["things done well"]
}

Rules:
- Only use "request_changes" for genuine bugs, security issues, or broken functionality
- Style preferences and minor improvements should be "nits" or "important", not blocking
- Be specific — reference file names and line numbers
- Output ONLY the JSON, no markdown fences, no extra text
REVIEW_EOF

  # Spawn Claude in plan mode (read-only) for the review
  local REVIEW_OUTPUT
  REVIEW_OUTPUT=$(claude --permission-mode plan --print -p "$(cat "$REVIEW_PROMPT")" --max-turns 20 2>/dev/null)
  rm -f "$REVIEW_PROMPT"

  if [ -z "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" ]; then
    log "[review] Claude returned empty output for PR #${PR_NUMBER}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Try to parse as JSON
  local VERDICT SUMMARY REVIEW_BODY
  VERDICT=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.verdict // empty' 2>/dev/null)

  if [ -n "$VERDICT" ]; then
    SUMMARY=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.summary // ""' 2>/dev/null)
    local BLOCKING IMPORTANT NITS PRAISE
    BLOCKING=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.blocking // [] | map("- **BLOCKING**: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)
    IMPORTANT=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.important // [] | map("- **Important**: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)
    NITS=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.nits // [] | map("- _Nit_: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)
    PRAISE=$(echo "$REVIEW_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.praise // [] | map("- :thumbsup: " + .) | join("\n")' 2>/dev/null)

    REVIEW_BODY="## Auto-Review: ${SUMMARY}

${BLOCKING}
${IMPORTANT}
${NITS}
${PRAISE}

---
_Automated review by claude-agent-runner_"

    # Post review via gh
    case "$VERDICT" in
      approve)
        gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --approve --body "$REVIEW_BODY" 2>/dev/null
        log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} APPROVED"
        ;;
      request_changes)
        gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --request-changes --body "$REVIEW_BODY" 2>/dev/null
        log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} CHANGES REQUESTED"
        ;;
      *)
        gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --comment --body "$REVIEW_BODY" 2>/dev/null
        log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} COMMENTED"
        ;;
    esac
  else
    # Non-JSON output — post as plain comment
    gh pr review "$PR_NUMBER" --comment --body "## Auto-Review

${REVIEW_OUTPUT}

---
_Automated review by claude-agent-runner_" 2>/dev/null
    VERDICT="comment"
    log "[review] PR #${PR_NUMBER} commented (non-JSON output)"
  fi

  echo "$VERDICT"
}

# --- Auto-fix loop (Feature 3) ---

auto_fix_loop() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local REPO_DIR="$2"
  local BRANCH="$3"
  local IDENTIFIER="$4"
  local REPO_NAME="$5"

  cd "$WORKTREE" || return 1

  local CI_GATE
  CI_GATE=$(resolve_ci_gate)

  for ITER in $(seq 1 "$MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS"); do
    log "[auto-fix] Iteration ${ITER}/${MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS} for ${IDENTIFIER}"

    # Check latest review verdict
    local LATEST_REVIEW_STATE
    LATEST_REVIEW_STATE=$(cd "$REPO_DIR" && gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews --jq '[.reviews[] | select(.state != "")] | last | .state // "NONE"' 2>/dev/null)

    if [ "$LATEST_REVIEW_STATE" = "APPROVED" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] PR already approved, stopping fix loop"
      return 0
    fi

    if [ "$LATEST_REVIEW_STATE" != "CHANGES_REQUESTED" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] No changes requested (state: ${LATEST_REVIEW_STATE}), stopping fix loop"
      return 0
    fi

    # Gather all feedback
    local PR_FEEDBACK
    PR_FEEDBACK=$(gather_pr_feedback "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH")

    if [ -z "$PR_FEEDBACK" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] No feedback to address, stopping"
      return 0
    fi

    # Spawn fix agent
    cd "$WORKTREE" || return 1

    # Export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE for guardrail hook
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_BRANCH="$BRANCH"
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
    export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"

    claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions.

## Fix PR Feedback: ${IDENTIFIER}

Address ALL reviewer feedback below. Make new, atomic commits for each fix.
Do NOT amend existing commits or force-push.
Do NOT push or open a PR — that is handled externally.

${PR_FEEDBACK}

Fix every issue mentioned above." --max-turns 500 >> "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-${ITER}.log" 2>&1

    unset CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE

    # Run CI gate
    if [ -n "$CI_GATE" ] && [ -x "$CI_GATE" ]; then
      if ! "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >> "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-ci-${ITER}.log" 2>&1; then
        log "[auto-fix] CI failed after fix iteration ${ITER}"
        # One CI retry within the fix
        claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "CI gate failed after fixing PR feedback. Fix the CI errors:

$(cat "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-ci-${ITER}.log" | tail -50)

Make new commits. Do NOT push." --max-turns 200 >> "$LOG_DIR/${IDENTIFIER}-fix-ci-retry-${ITER}.log" 2>&1

        if ! "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          log "[auto-fix] CI still failing after retry, bailing out of fix loop"
          return 1
        fi
      fi
    fi

    # Push new commits
    cd "$WORKTREE" || return 1
    git push origin "$BRANCH" 2>&1

    # Re-run auto-review
    local NEW_VERDICT
    NEW_VERDICT=$(auto_review_pr "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER")

    if [ "$NEW_VERDICT" = "approve" ]; then
      log "[auto-fix] PR approved after fix iteration ${ITER}"
      return 0
    fi

    log "[auto-fix] Review verdict after fix ${ITER}: ${NEW_VERDICT}"
  done

  log "[auto-fix] Exhausted ${MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS} fix iterations for ${IDENTIFIER}"
  return 1
}

# --- Orchestrator (multi-agent decomposition) ---

# Check if an issue should be orchestrated (by label)
should_orchestrate() {
  local ISSUE_JSON="$1"

  # Check if the issue has the orchestrate label
  local HAS_ORCH_LABEL
  HAS_ORCH_LABEL=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | jq -r --arg label "$ORCH_LABEL" \
    '.labels.nodes[]? | select(.name == $label) | .name' 2>/dev/null)

  [ -n "$HAS_ORCH_LABEL" ]
}

# Check if an issue is an orchestrated subtask (has a parent issue)
is_orchestrated_subtask() {
  local ISSUE_JSON="$1"

  local HAS_PARENT
  HAS_PARENT=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | jq -r '.parent.id // empty' 2>/dev/null)

  [ -n "$HAS_PARENT" ]
}

# Orchestrate a parent issue: decompose into sub-issues and supervise
orchestrate_issue() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local REPO_DIR="$2"
  local IDENTIFIER="$3"
  local TITLE="$4"
  local DESCRIPTION="$5"
  local API_KEY="$6"
  local ISSUE_ID="$7"
  local TEAM_ID="$8"
  local WORKSPACE="$9"
  local TEAM_NAME="${10}"
  local SLACK_WEBHOOK="${11}"
  local REPO_NAME
  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")

  local ORCH_DIR="$WORKTREE/.claude/orchestrator"
  mkdir -p "$ORCH_DIR"

  log "[orchestrator] Decomposing ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}"
  linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 **Orchestrator started** — decomposing into subtasks" >/dev/null 2>&1
  slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🧩 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Orchestrator started for ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}"

  cd "$WORKTREE"

  # Phase 1: Spawn Claude to decompose the ticket
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"
  unset CLAUDECODE 2>/dev/null || true

  claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are an orchestrator decomposing a Linear issue into subtasks.

Read CLAUDE.md for project conventions and architecture.

## Parent Issue: ${IDENTIFIER}
**Title:** ${TITLE}

**Description:**
${DESCRIPTION}

## Instructions

Use the orchestrate skill to decompose this issue into scoped subtasks.
Write subtask JSON files to .claude/orchestrator/subtask-NNN.json

If this issue is single-concern and doesn't need decomposition, write:
{\"no_decomposition\": true, \"reason\": \"...\"}
to .claude/orchestrator/no_decomposition.json

Max subtasks: ${ORCH_MAX_SUBTASKS}

Explore the codebase thoroughly before decomposing. Every file path in your scope assignments must be a real path." --max-turns "$ORCH_MAX_TURNS" >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-orchestrate.log" 2>&1

  # Check for no-decomposition result
  if [ -f "$ORCH_DIR/no_decomposition.json" ]; then
    log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} — no decomposition needed"
    linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 Orchestrator decided no decomposition needed — falling back to single agent" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1  # Signal caller to fall back to single-agent
  fi

  # Read subtask files
  local SUBTASK_COUNT=0
  local SUBTASK_FILES=()
  for f in "$ORCH_DIR"/subtask-*.json; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    if jq empty "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
      SUBTASK_FILES+=("$f")
      SUBTASK_COUNT=$((SUBTASK_COUNT + 1))
    else
      log "[orchestrator] WARNING: Invalid JSON in $(basename "$f"), skipping"
    fi
  done

  if [ "$SUBTASK_COUNT" -le 1 ]; then
    log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} — ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks (need 2+), falling back to single agent"
    linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 Orchestrator produced ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtask(s) — falling back to single agent" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1
  fi

  log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks"

  # Get agent label ID
  local AGENT_LABEL_ID
  AGENT_LABEL_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issueLabels(filter: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }) { nodes { id } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issueLabels.nodes[0].id // empty')

  local LABEL_IDS="[]"
  [ -n "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ] && LABEL_IDS="[\"$AGENT_LABEL_ID\"]"

  # Phase 2: Create sub-issues in Linear
  # Build state.json as we go
  local STATE_SUBTASKS="[]"

  for subtask_file in "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}"; do
    local ST_ID ST_TITLE ST_SKILL ST_SCOPE ST_OFF_LIMITS ST_SHARED ST_DEPS ST_CRITERIA ST_CONTEXT
    ST_ID=$(jq -r '.id' "$subtask_file")
    ST_TITLE=$(jq -r '.title' "$subtask_file")
    ST_SKILL=$(jq -r '.skill // "implement"' "$subtask_file")
    ST_SCOPE=$(jq -r '.scope | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")
    ST_CRITERIA=$(jq -r '.acceptance_criteria | map("- " + .) | join("\n")' "$subtask_file")
    ST_CONTEXT=$(jq -r '.context // ""' "$subtask_file")
    ST_DEPS=$(jq -r '.depends_on // [] | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")

    # Build sub-issue description with scope metadata
    local SUB_DESC="## Subtask of ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}

${ST_CONTEXT}

## File Scope

**Allowed (write):** ${ST_SCOPE}

**Off limits:** $(jq -r '.off_limits | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")

**Shared files (read-only):** $(jq -r '.shared_files // [] | join(", ")' "$subtask_file")

## Acceptance Criteria

${ST_CRITERIA}

## Dependencies

${ST_DEPS:-None}
"

    # Create the sub-issue
    local CREATE_RESULT
    CREATE_RESULT=$(linear_create_sub_issue "$API_KEY" "$TEAM_ID" "$ISSUE_ID" \
      "[${ST_ID}] ${ST_TITLE}" "$SUB_DESC" "$LABEL_IDS")

    local SUB_ISSUE_ID SUB_IDENTIFIER
    SUB_ISSUE_ID=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.id // empty')
    SUB_IDENTIFIER=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.identifier // empty')

    if [ -n "$SUB_ISSUE_ID" ]; then
      log "[orchestrator] Created subtask: ${SUB_IDENTIFIER} — ${ST_TITLE}"

      # Track in state
      STATE_SUBTASKS=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq \
        --arg id "$ST_ID" \
        --arg linear_id "$SUB_ISSUE_ID" \
        --arg linear_ident "$SUB_IDENTIFIER" \
        --arg skill "$ST_SKILL" \
        '. + [{"id": $id, "linear_id": $linear_id, "linear_identifier": $linear_ident, "status": "pending", "skill": $skill}]')
    else
      log "[orchestrator] ERROR: Failed to create subtask: ${ST_TITLE}"
    fi
  done

  # Create dependency relations between sub-issues
  for subtask_file in "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}"; do
    local ST_ID DEPS
    ST_ID=$(jq -r '.id' "$subtask_file")
    DEPS=$(jq -r '.depends_on[]?' "$subtask_file" 2>/dev/null)
    [ -z "$DEPS" ] && continue

    # Find this subtask's Linear ID
    local THIS_LINEAR_ID
    THIS_LINEAR_ID=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -r --arg id "$ST_ID" '.[] | select(.id == $id) | .linear_id')
    [ -z "$THIS_LINEAR_ID" ] && continue

    # Don't add agent label to subtasks with dependencies (they'll get it when unblocked)
    if [ -n "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ]; then
      linear_remove_label "$API_KEY" "$THIS_LINEAR_ID" "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
    fi

    while IFS= read -r dep_id; do
      local BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID
      BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -r --arg id "$dep_id" '.[] | select(.id == $id) | .linear_id')
      if [ -n "$BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID" ]; then
        linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
          issueRelationCreate(input: {
            issueId: \"$THIS_LINEAR_ID\"
            relatedIssueId: \"$BLOCKER_LINEAR_ID\"
            type: \"isBlockedBy\"
          }) { success }
        }" >/dev/null 2>&1
        log "[orchestrator] Linked: subtask ${ST_ID} blocked by ${dep_id}"
      fi
    done <<< "$DEPS"
  done

  # Write state.json
  local SHARED_FILES
  SHARED_FILES=$(jq -s '[.[].shared_files // [] | .[]] | unique' "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}" 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")

  jq -n \
    --arg parent_id "$IDENTIFIER" \
    --arg branch "agent/${IDENTIFIER}" \
    --arg issue_id "$ISSUE_ID" \
    --argjson subtasks "$STATE_SUBTASKS" \
    --argjson shared_files "$SHARED_FILES" \
    '{
      parent_id: $parent_id,
      branch: $branch,
      issue_id: $issue_id,
      subtasks: $subtasks,
      shared_files: $shared_files,
      created_at: (now | todate),
      status: "active"
    }' > "$ORCH_DIR/state.json"

  # Post summary to parent issue
  local SUBTASK_SUMMARY=""
  for subtask_file in "${SUBTASK_FILES[@]}"; do
    local ST_ID ST_TITLE
    ST_ID=$(jq -r '.id' "$subtask_file")
    ST_TITLE=$(jq -r '.title' "$subtask_file")
    local ST_IDENT
    ST_IDENT=$(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -r --arg id "$ST_ID" '.[] | select(.id == $id) | .linear_identifier')
    SUBTASK_SUMMARY="${SUBTASK_SUMMARY}
- **${ST_IDENT}** [${ST_ID}] ${ST_TITLE}"
  done

  linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🧩 **Orchestrator decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks:**
${SUBTASK_SUMMARY}

Workers will pick up subtasks as they become unblocked. The orchestrator will validate results and create a combined PR." >/dev/null 2>&1

  # Commit the orchestrator state
  cd "$WORKTREE"
  git add .claude/orchestrator/ 2>/dev/null
  git commit -m "chore: orchestrator decomposition for ${IDENTIFIER}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Phase 3: Supervisor loop — poll for sub-issue completion
  log "[orchestrator] Entering supervisor loop for ${IDENTIFIER}"

  while true; do
    sleep "$ORCH_POLL_INTERVAL"

    # Read current state
    if [ ! -f "$ORCH_DIR/state.json" ]; then
      log "[orchestrator] ERROR: state.json missing, aborting"
      break
    fi

    # Check sub-issue states in Linear
    local ALL_DONE=true
    local ANY_FAILED=false
    local UPDATED_SUBTASKS="$STATE_SUBTASKS"

    for subtask_entry in $(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -c '.[]'); do
      local ST_ID ST_LINEAR_ID ST_STATUS
      ST_ID=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.id')
      ST_LINEAR_ID=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.linear_id')
      ST_STATUS=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.status')

      # Skip already-completed subtasks
      [ "$ST_STATUS" = "completed" ] && continue
      [ "$ST_STATUS" = "validated" ] && continue

      # Query Linear for current state
      local LINEAR_STATE
      LINEAR_STATE=$(linear_get_issue_state "$API_KEY" "$ST_LINEAR_ID")

      case "$LINEAR_STATE" in
        completed)
          log "[orchestrator] Subtask ${ST_ID} completed — validating"
          UPDATED_SUBTASKS=$(echo "$UPDATED_SUBTASKS" | jq --arg id "$ST_ID" \
            '[.[] | if .id == $id then .status = "completed" else . end]')

          # Check for scope overflow
          local OVERFLOW_FILE="$ORCH_DIR/scope-overflow-${ST_ID}.md"
          if [ -f "$OVERFLOW_FILE" ]; then
            log "[orchestrator] Subtask ${ST_ID} has scope overflow — creating follow-up"
            local OVERFLOW_CONTENT
            OVERFLOW_CONTENT=$(cat "$OVERFLOW_FILE")
            linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "⚠️ Subtask ${ST_ID} reported scope overflow:

${OVERFLOW_CONTENT}

Creating a follow-up subtask." >/dev/null 2>&1
            # Overflow handling: could create another sub-issue here
            # For now, log it for the orchestrator's final validation
          fi
          ;;
        canceled)
          log "[orchestrator] Subtask ${ST_ID} was canceled"
          UPDATED_SUBTASKS=$(echo "$UPDATED_SUBTASKS" | jq --arg id "$ST_ID" \
            '[.[] | if .id == $id then .status = "canceled" else . end]')
          ;;
        *)
          ALL_DONE=false
          ;;
      esac
    done

    STATE_SUBTASKS="$UPDATED_SUBTASKS"

    # Update state.json
    jq --argjson subtasks "$STATE_SUBTASKS" '.subtasks = $subtasks' "$ORCH_DIR/state.json" > "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" \
      && mv "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" "$ORCH_DIR/state.json"

    if [ "$ALL_DONE" = true ]; then
      break
    fi
  done

  log "[orchestrator] All subtasks completed for ${IDENTIFIER} — running integration validation"

  # Phase 4: Integration validation
  cd "$WORKTREE"

  # Pull latest changes from sub-issue workers (they commit to the same branch)
  git pull origin "agent/${IDENTIFIER}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Run CI gate
  local CI_GATE
  CI_GATE=$(resolve_ci_gate)

  local INTEGRATION_PASSED=false
  if [ -n "$CI_GATE" ] && [ -x "$CI_GATE" ]; then
    if "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration.log" 2>&1; then
      INTEGRATION_PASSED=true
      log "[orchestrator] Integration CI passed for ${IDENTIFIER}"
    else
      log "[orchestrator] Integration CI failed for ${IDENTIFIER} — spawning fix agent"

      local CI_ERRORS
      CI_ERRORS=$(cat "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration.log" | tail -80)

      claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for project conventions.

## Integration CI Failed — Fix These Errors

This is an orchestrated feature (${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}) with multiple subtasks that have been completed.
The integration CI gate failed. Fix ALL errors below.

## CI Output:
\`\`\`
${CI_ERRORS}
\`\`\`

Commit fixes with: fix: resolve integration CI failures for ${IDENTIFIER}
Do NOT push or open a PR." --max-turns 500 >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration-fix.log" 2>&1

      # Retry CI
      if "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" >> "$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration-retry.log" 2>&1; then
        INTEGRATION_PASSED=true
        log "[orchestrator] Integration CI passed after fix for ${IDENTIFIER}"
      else
        log "[orchestrator] Integration CI still failing for ${IDENTIFIER}"
      fi
    fi
  else
    INTEGRATION_PASSED=true  # No CI gate = pass
  fi

  # Phase 5: Create PR
  if [ "$INTEGRATION_PASSED" = true ]; then
    local BRANCH="agent/${IDENTIFIER}"
    git push -u origin "$BRANCH" 2>&1

    # Build PR body from all agent reports
    local PR_BODY="## ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}

### Orchestrated Implementation

This feature was decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks by the orchestrator.

### Subtask Reports
"
    for subtask_entry in $(echo "$STATE_SUBTASKS" | jq -c '.[]'); do
      local ST_ID ST_IDENT
      ST_ID=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.id')
      ST_IDENT=$(echo "$subtask_entry" | jq -r '.linear_identifier')
      local REPORT_FILE="$WORKTREE/.claude/agent-reports/${ST_IDENT}.md"
      if [ -f "$REPORT_FILE" ]; then
        PR_BODY="${PR_BODY}
#### ${ST_IDENT} (subtask ${ST_ID})
$(cat "$REPORT_FILE")

"
      fi
    done

    PR_BODY="${PR_BODY}
---
*Implemented by orchestrated Claude agents. CI gate passed.*
Closes ${IDENTIFIER}"

    local PR_URL
    PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
      --title "${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}" \
      --body "$PR_BODY" \
      --head "$BRANCH" \
      --base main 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -oE 'https://github\.com/[^ ]+')

    if [ -n "$PR_URL" ]; then
      linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "In Review" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
      linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "✅ **Orchestrator completed** — all ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks done, CI passed, PR opened: ${PR_URL}" >/dev/null 2>&1
      slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "✅ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} completed — ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks, PR: ${PR_URL}"
      log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} done — PR: $PR_URL"
    else
      linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "⚠️ **Orchestrator push succeeded but PR creation failed** — check logs" >/dev/null 2>&1
      log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} pushed but PR creation failed"
    fi
  else
    linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "❌ **Orchestrator integration CI failed** — needs human intervention.

Check logs: ${LOG_DIR}/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-integration*.log" >/dev/null 2>&1
    slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "❌ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} integration CI failed — needs human intervention"
    log "[orchestrator] ${IDENTIFIER} integration failed"
  fi

  # Update state
  jq '.status = "completed"' "$ORCH_DIR/state.json" > "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" \
    && mv "$ORCH_DIR/state.json.tmp" "$ORCH_DIR/state.json"

  return 0
}

# Run a scoped worker for an orchestrated subtask
run_scoped_worker() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local REPO_NAME="$2"
  local IDENTIFIER="$3"
  local TITLE="$4"
  local DESCRIPTION="$5"

  # Find the subtask spec embedded in the description
  local SCOPE_SECTION
  SCOPE_SECTION=$(echo "$DESCRIPTION" | sed -n '/## File Scope/,/## Acceptance/p' | head -n -1)

  local CRITERIA_SECTION
  CRITERIA_SECTION=$(echo "$DESCRIPTION" | sed -n '/## Acceptance Criteria/,/## Dependencies/p' | head -n -1)

  # Create a temporary scope file for the scope-guard hook
  local SCOPE_FILE="$WORKTREE/.claude/orchestrator/active-scope.json"
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$SCOPE_FILE")"

  # Extract scope from the description — parse "Allowed (write):" line
  local WRITE_SCOPE
  WRITE_SCOPE=$(echo "$SCOPE_SECTION" | grep -A1 'Allowed (write):' | tail -1 | sed 's/^ *//')

  # Build a simple scope JSON from the comma-separated list
  echo "$WRITE_SCOPE" | tr ',' '\n' | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//' | jq -R . | jq -s '{scope: .}' > "$SCOPE_FILE"

  # Set scoped worker environment
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPED=1
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPE_FILE="$SCOPE_FILE"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
  export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"

  unset CLAUDECODE 2>/dev/null || true

  claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are a scoped worker in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions.

## Your Subtask: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}

Use the scoped-worker skill for your implementation workflow.

${DESCRIPTION}

## IMPORTANT
- Your file writes are enforced by the scope-guard hook — stay within your scope
- If you need out-of-scope changes, write to .claude/orchestrator/scope-overflow report
- Do NOT push or open PRs — the orchestrator handles that
- Commit frequently with conventional commit messages
- Create your agent report at .claude/agent-reports/${IDENTIFIER}.md" --max-turns 500

  unset CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPED
  unset CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPE_FILE
}

# --- Pipeline command (Feature 1) ---

run_pipeline() {
  local REQUIREMENTS_FILE="$1"
  local WORKSPACE="${2:-}"
  local TEAM_NAME="${3:-}"
  local DRY_RUN="${4:-false}"

  if [ ! -f "$REQUIREMENTS_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Requirements file not found: $REQUIREMENTS_FILE"
    exit 1
  fi

  local REQUIREMENTS
  REQUIREMENTS=$(cat "$REQUIREMENTS_FILE")

  # Default to first workspace/team if not specified
  if [ -z "$WORKSPACE" ]; then
    WORKSPACE=$(jq -r '.workspaces | keys[0]' "$CONFIG")
  fi
  if [ -z "$TEAM_NAME" ]; then
    TEAM_NAME=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].teams | keys[0]" "$CONFIG")
  fi

  local API_KEY
  API_KEY=$(resolve_api_key "$WORKSPACE") || exit 1

  log "Parsing requirements into stories..."

  # Spawn Claude to parse requirements into structured JSON
  local PARSE_OUTPUT
  PARSE_OUTPUT=$(claude --print --max-turns 3 --output-format json -p "Parse the following product requirements into structured development stories.

Output ONLY a JSON array with this structure:
[
  {
    \"title\": \"Short imperative title (e.g., 'Add user authentication')\",
    \"description\": \"Detailed description with acceptance criteria\",
    \"estimate\": 1,
    \"labels\": [\"feature\"],
    \"dependencies\": []
  }
]

Rules:
- estimate is in story points (1=trivial, 2=small, 3=medium, 5=large, 8=very large)
- labels: use 'feature', 'bug', 'chore', 'improvement'
- dependencies: list titles of other stories this one depends on (must complete first)
- Break large requirements into small, independent stories
- Each story should be completable in one agent session (1-3 hours of work)
- Output ONLY the JSON array, nothing else

## Requirements

${REQUIREMENTS}" 2>/dev/null)

  if [ -z "$PARSE_OUTPUT" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Claude returned empty output while parsing requirements"
    exit 1
  fi

  # Validate JSON
  if ! echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: Claude returned invalid JSON:"
    echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT"
    exit 1
  fi

  local STORY_COUNT
  STORY_COUNT=$(echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq length)
  log "Parsed $STORY_COUNT stories"

  # Dry run — just print
  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "=== Pipeline Dry Run ==="
    echo "Workspace: $WORKSPACE | Team: $TEAM_NAME"
    echo "Stories: $STORY_COUNT"
    echo ""
    echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.[] | "[\(.estimate)pt] \(.title)\n  Labels: \(.labels | join(", "))\n  Deps: \(.dependencies | if length > 0 then join(", ") else "none" end)\n  \(.description)\n"'
    return 0
  fi

  # Get team ID
  local TEAM_ID
  TEAM_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ teams(filter: { name: { eq: \"$TEAM_NAME\" } }) { nodes { id } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.teams.nodes[0].id // empty')

  if [ -z "$TEAM_ID" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Team '$TEAM_NAME' not found in Linear"
    exit 1
  fi

  # Get label ID for the agent label
  local AGENT_LABEL_ID
  AGENT_LABEL_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issueLabels(filter: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }) { nodes { id } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issueLabels.nodes[0].id // empty')

  if [ -z "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Label '$LABEL' not found in Linear — issues will be created without it"
  fi

  # Create issues
  log "Creating $STORY_COUNT Linear issues..."

  # Map title -> issue ID for dependency resolution
  declare -A TITLE_TO_ID

  echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r story; do
    local STORY_TITLE STORY_DESC STORY_ESTIMATE
    STORY_TITLE=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.title')
    STORY_DESC=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.description')
    STORY_ESTIMATE=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.estimate // 0')

    local LABEL_IDS=""
    if [ -n "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ]; then
      # Check if this story has dependencies and --sequenced is set
      local HAS_DEPS
      HAS_DEPS=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.dependencies | length')

      if [ "$SEQUENCED" = true ] && [ "$HAS_DEPS" -gt 0 ]; then
        # Don't add agent label — will be added when blockers complete
        LABEL_IDS="[]"
      else
        LABEL_IDS="[\"$AGENT_LABEL_ID\"]"
      fi
    else
      LABEL_IDS="[]"
    fi

    local CREATE_RESULT
    CREATE_RESULT=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
      issueCreate(input: {
        teamId: \"$TEAM_ID\"
        title: $(echo "$STORY_TITLE" | jq -Rs .)
        description: $(echo "$STORY_DESC" | jq -Rs .)
        estimate: $STORY_ESTIMATE
        labelIds: $LABEL_IDS
      }) {
        success
        issue { id identifier }
      }
    }")

    local NEW_ID NEW_IDENT
    NEW_ID=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.id // empty')
    NEW_IDENT=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.identifier // empty')

    if [ -n "$NEW_ID" ]; then
      log "Created: ${NEW_IDENT} — ${STORY_TITLE}"
      TITLE_TO_ID["$STORY_TITLE"]="$NEW_ID"
    else
      log "ERROR: Failed to create issue: ${STORY_TITLE}"
    fi
  done

  # Create dependency relations (second pass)
  echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r story; do
    local STORY_TITLE
    STORY_TITLE=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.title')
    local DEPS
    DEPS=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.dependencies[]?' 2>/dev/null)

    [ -z "$DEPS" ] && continue

    local THIS_ID="${TITLE_TO_ID[$STORY_TITLE]:-}"
    [ -z "$THIS_ID" ] && continue

    while IFS= read -r dep_title; do
      local BLOCKER_ID="${TITLE_TO_ID[$dep_title]:-}"
      if [ -n "$BLOCKER_ID" ]; then
        linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
          issueRelationCreate(input: {
            issueId: \"$THIS_ID\"
            relatedIssueId: \"$BLOCKER_ID\"
            type: \"isBlockedBy\"
          }) { success }
        }" >/dev/null 2>&1
        log "  Linked: ${STORY_TITLE} blocked by ${dep_title}"
      fi
    done <<< "$DEPS"
  done

  log "Pipeline complete — $STORY_COUNT issues created in ${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}"
}

# --- Spec command (Feature 2) ---

run_spec() {
  local IDEA_FILE="$1"
  local WORKSPACE="${2:-}"
  local TEAM_NAME="${3:-}"
  local INTERACTIVE="${4:-false}"
  local CREATE="${5:-false}"

  if [ ! -f "$IDEA_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Idea file not found: $IDEA_FILE"
    exit 1
  fi

  local IDEA
  IDEA=$(cat "$IDEA_FILE")

  # Default to first workspace/team if not specified
  if [ -z "$WORKSPACE" ]; then
    WORKSPACE=$(jq -r '.workspaces | keys[0]' "$CONFIG")
  fi
  if [ -z "$TEAM_NAME" ]; then
    TEAM_NAME=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].teams | keys[0]" "$CONFIG")
  fi

  log "Generating spec from idea..."

  # Determine project repo for context
  local REPO_DIR
  REPO_DIR=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].teams[\"$TEAM_NAME\"].repo // empty" "$CONFIG")

  local CONTEXT_PROMPT=""
  if [ -n "$REPO_DIR" ] && [ -d "$REPO_DIR" ]; then
    local CLAUDE_MD=""
    [ -f "$REPO_DIR/CLAUDE.md" ] && CLAUDE_MD=$(cat "$REPO_DIR/CLAUDE.md")
    if [ -n "$CLAUDE_MD" ]; then
      CONTEXT_PROMPT="
## Project Context (from CLAUDE.md)
${CLAUDE_MD}
"
    fi
  fi

  local SPEC_PROMPT="Use the spec-writer skill to transform this idea into structured development requirements.
${CONTEXT_PROMPT}
## Idea

${IDEA}

## Output Format

Output a markdown document compatible with the --pipeline command:
- Each story has a title, estimate, labels, dependencies, and description
- Stories should be sized for single agent sessions (1-3 hours)
- Include acceptance criteria for each story
- Note which stories should get the 'Orchestrate' label for multi-agent decomposition"

  if [ "$INTERACTIVE" = true ]; then
    # Interactive mode — let Claude ask questions via stdin/stdout
    log "Running spec-writer in interactive mode..."
    if [ -n "$REPO_DIR" ] && [ -d "$REPO_DIR" ]; then
      cd "$REPO_DIR"
    fi
    claude --permission-mode plan --setting-sources project,local -p "$SPEC_PROMPT"
  else
    # Non-interactive — generate spec and output
    local SPEC_OUTPUT
    SPEC_OUTPUT=$(claude --print --permission-mode plan --setting-sources project,local -p "$SPEC_PROMPT" --max-turns 20 2>/dev/null)

    if [ -z "$SPEC_OUTPUT" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Claude returned empty output while generating spec"
      exit 1
    fi

    echo "$SPEC_OUTPUT"

    # If --create: save to temp file and chain to pipeline
    if [ "$CREATE" = true ]; then
      local SPEC_FILE
      SPEC_FILE=$(mktemp /tmp/spec-XXXXXX.md)
      echo "$SPEC_OUTPUT" > "$SPEC_FILE"
      log "Spec written to $SPEC_FILE — chaining to pipeline..."
      run_pipeline "$SPEC_FILE" "$WORKSPACE" "$TEAM_NAME" false
      rm -f "$SPEC_FILE"
    fi
  fi
}

# --- Main agent loop ---

run_agents_for_team() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local WORKSPACE="$2"
  local TEAM_NAME="$3"
  local REPO_DIR="$4"
  local LOCAL_DB="${5:-none}"
  local REPO_NAME
  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
  local SLACK_WEBHOOK
  SLACK_WEBHOOK=$(resolve_slack_webhook "$WORKSPACE")

  # Lock per repo
  local LOCK_FILE="$LOCK_DIR/${REPO_NAME}.lock"
  if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
    local LOCK_PID
    LOCK_PID=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE")
    if kill -0 "$LOCK_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
      log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Already running (PID $LOCK_PID), skipping"
      return 0
    else
      rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
    fi
  fi
  echo $$ > "$LOCK_FILE"

  cd "$REPO_DIR" || { log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Repo not found: $REPO_DIR"; rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"; return 1; }

  cleanup_worktrees "$REPO_DIR"

  # Count active slots by running agent processes (not just worktree dirs)
  # A worktree sitting idle (PR open, waiting for review) doesn't count
  local ACTIVE=0
  local WT_BASE="$HOME/.claude/worktrees/${REPO_NAME}"
  if [ -d "$WT_BASE" ]; then
    for wt in "$WT_BASE"/issue-*; do
      [ -d "$wt" ] || continue
      local PID_FILE="$wt/.claude/agent.pid"
      if [ -f "$PID_FILE" ]; then
        local AGENT_PID
        AGENT_PID=$(cat "$PID_FILE")
        if kill -0 "$AGENT_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
          ACTIVE=$((ACTIVE + 1))
        else
          # Stale PID file — agent finished
          rm -f "$PID_FILE"
        fi
      fi
    done
  fi

  local SLOTS=$((MAX_PARALLEL - ACTIVE))
  if [ "$SLOTS" -le 0 ]; then
    log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] All $MAX_PARALLEL slots in use ($ACTIVE agents running)"
    rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
    return 0
  fi

  # Fetch issues from Linear
  local ISSUES
  ISSUES=$(fetch_agent_issues "$API_KEY" "$TEAM_NAME" "$SLOTS")
  local COUNT
  COUNT=$(echo "$ISSUES" | jq length)

  if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] No '$LABEL' issues"
    rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
    return 0
  fi

  log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Found $COUNT issues, $SLOTS slots available"

  # Get the label ID for removal
  local LABEL_ID
  LABEL_ID=$(echo "$ISSUES" | jq -r ".[0].labels.nodes[] | select(.name == \"$LABEL\") | .id" 2>/dev/null)

  # Resolve ci-gate path once
  local CI_GATE
  CI_GATE=$(resolve_ci_gate)

  echo "$ISSUES" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r issue; do
    local ISSUE_ID IDENTIFIER TITLE DESCRIPTION TEAM_ID BRANCH WORKTREE
    ISSUE_ID=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.id')
    IDENTIFIER=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.identifier')
    TITLE=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.title')
    DESCRIPTION=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.description // ""')
    TEAM_ID=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.team.id')

    BRANCH="agent/${IDENTIFIER}"
    WORKTREE_BASE="$HOME/.claude/worktrees/${REPO_NAME}"
    mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_BASE"
    WORKTREE="$WORKTREE_BASE/issue-${IDENTIFIER}"

    RESUMING=false
    RESUME_REASON=""
    if [ -d "$WORKTREE" ]; then
      BLOCKED_FILE="$WORKTREE/.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md"
      if [ -f "$BLOCKED_FILE" ]; then
        RESUMING=true
        RESUME_REASON="blocked"
        log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Resuming ${IDENTIFIER} (previously blocked)"
        rm -f "$BLOCKED_FILE"
      else
        RESUMING=true
        RESUME_REASON="feedback"
        log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Resuming ${IDENTIFIER} (feedback on existing PR)"
      fi
    else
      git fetch origin main 2>/dev/null || true
      git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH" origin/main 2>/dev/null || {
        log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Failed to create worktree for ${IDENTIFIER}"
        linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "❌ **Agent failed** — could not create worktree" >/dev/null 2>&1
        continue
      }

      [ -f ".env" ] && cp .env "$WORKTREE/.env"
      [ -f ".env.local" ] && cp .env.local "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
    fi

    # --- Linear: In Progress ---
    linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "In Progress" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
    linear_remove_label "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "$LABEL_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1

    # Start DB container if configured
    if [ "$LOCAL_DB" != "none" ]; then
      start_agent_db "$WORKTREE" "$IDENTIFIER" "$REPO_DIR" "$LOCAL_DB"
    fi

    local ISSUE_LOG="$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}.log"

    (
      cd "$WORKTREE"

      # Export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE for guardrail hook
      export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
      export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
      export CLAUDE_AGENT_BRANCH="$BRANCH"
      export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
      export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"

      # Track transient Linear comment IDs for cleanup
      TRANSIENT_COMMENTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-comments.tmp"
      : > "$TRANSIENT_COMMENTS_FILE"

      post_status() {
        local CID
        CID=$(linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "$1")
        if [ -n "$CID" ]; then
          echo "$CID" >> "$TRANSIENT_COMMENTS_FILE"
        fi
      }

      cleanup_status_comments() {
        if [ -f "$TRANSIENT_COMMENTS_FILE" ]; then
          while IFS= read -r CID; do
            [ -n "$CID" ] && linear_delete_comment "$API_KEY" "$CID"
          done < "$TRANSIENT_COMMENTS_FILE"
          rm -f "$TRANSIENT_COMMENTS_FILE"
        fi
      }

      # Install dependencies (skip on resume)
      if [ "$RESUMING" != true ]; then
        if [ -f "pnpm-lock.yaml" ]; then
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || pnpm install 2>/dev/null
        fi
        if [ -f "dashboard/pnpm-lock.yaml" ]; then
          (cd dashboard && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || pnpm install 2>/dev/null)
        fi
      fi

      # Unset CLAUDECODE to allow spawning Claude from within a Claude session
      unset CLAUDECODE 2>/dev/null || true

      # Session ID file for resume support
      SESSION_FILE="$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-session.id"

      if [ "$RESUMING" = true ]; then
        PREV_SESSION=$(cat "$SESSION_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        if [ "$RESUME_REASON" = "blocked" ]; then
          COMMENTS=$(linear_get_comments "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID")
          post_status "🔄 **Agent resuming** — developer responded, continuing work"
          slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🔄 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Agent resuming ${IDENTIFIER} — developer responded to blocked question"

          if [ -n "$PREV_SESSION" ]; then
            claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -r "$PREV_SESSION" -p "The developer has responded to your question. Here are the latest comments from Linear:

${COMMENTS}

Continue implementing ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}
Remember: do NOT push or open a PR. Just implement, test, and commit.
If you are still blocked, create .claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md again." --max-turns 500
          else
            RESUMING=false
          fi

        elif [ "$RESUME_REASON" = "feedback" ]; then
          COMMENTS=$(linear_get_comments "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID")
          PR_FEEDBACK=$(gather_pr_feedback "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH")
          post_status "🔄 **Agent resuming** — addressing reviewer feedback"
          slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🔄 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Agent resuming ${IDENTIFIER} — addressing PR/Linear feedback"

          FEEDBACK_PROMPT="You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions.

## Feedback Resume: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}

Reviewers have left feedback on your PR and/or the Linear ticket. Address ALL feedback below with new commits.
Do NOT amend existing commits or force-push. Make new, atomic commits for each fix.
Do NOT push or open a PR — that is handled externally.

"
          if [ -n "$PR_FEEDBACK" ]; then
            FEEDBACK_PROMPT="${FEEDBACK_PROMPT}## GitHub PR Feedback
${PR_FEEDBACK}
"
          fi

          if [ -n "$COMMENTS" ]; then
            FEEDBACK_PROMPT="${FEEDBACK_PROMPT}## Linear Ticket Comments
${COMMENTS}
"
          fi

          FEEDBACK_PROMPT="${FEEDBACK_PROMPT}
Address every piece of feedback above. If something is unclear, create .claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md with your questions."

          if [ -n "$PREV_SESSION" ]; then
            claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -r "$PREV_SESSION" -p "$FEEDBACK_PROMPT" --max-turns 500
          else
            claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "$FEEDBACK_PROMPT" --max-turns 500
          fi
        fi
      fi

      if [ "$RESUMING" != true ]; then

        # --- Orchestration detection ---
        if [ "$ORCH_ENABLED" = "true" ] && should_orchestrate "$issue"; then
          post_status "🧩 **Orchestrator starting** — decomposing into subtasks"
          slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🧩 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Orchestrator starting for ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}"

          if orchestrate_issue "$WORKTREE" "$REPO_DIR" "$IDENTIFIER" "$TITLE" "$DESCRIPTION" \
               "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "$TEAM_ID" "$WORKSPACE" "$TEAM_NAME" "$SLACK_WEBHOOK"; then
            # Orchestrator handled everything (CI, PR, Linear state)
            stop_agent_db "$IDENTIFIER"
            exit 0
          fi
          # Orchestrator returned 1 — fall through to single-agent flow
          log "[orchestrator] Falling back to single-agent for ${IDENTIFIER}"
        fi

        # --- Scoped worker detection ---
        if is_orchestrated_subtask "$issue"; then
          post_status "🔧 **Scoped worker** — implementing subtask"
          slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🔧 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Scoped worker started ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}"
          run_scoped_worker "$WORKTREE" "$REPO_NAME" "$IDENTIFIER" "$TITLE" "$DESCRIPTION"
        else

        # --- Standard single-agent flow ---
        post_status "🔧 **Agent working** — starting implementation"
        slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🔧 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Agent started ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}"

        claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions, architecture, and coding standards.

## Your Task
Implement the following Linear issue. Write tests where appropriate.
Make structured, atomic commits as you work — one commit per logical change
(e.g., separate commits for tests, implementation, docs, config changes).
Use conventional commit messages (feat:, fix:, test:, docs:, refactor:, chore:).
Do NOT save everything for one big commit at the end.

## IMPORTANT: Do NOT push or open a PR
The CI gate and PR creation are handled externally after you finish.
Just implement, test, and commit. Do NOT run git push or gh pr create.

## CRITICAL: If You Are Blocked or Need Clarification
If you cannot fully implement the feature without human input (unclear requirements,
pricing/business decisions, missing credentials, architectural choices that need approval, etc.):

YOU MUST:
1. Create the file \`.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md\` with your question(s)
2. \`git add\` and \`git commit\` it
3. STOP immediately — do NOT continue working, do NOT try to guess

DO NOT just write your questions to stdout. DO NOT skip this and push anyway.
The ONLY way to ask the developer a question is through the blocked file.
The system reads this file and posts it to Linear for the developer to answer.

If you have PARTIAL work done but still need answers, commit your partial work
AND the blocked file together.

## Linear Issue: ${IDENTIFIER}
**Title:** ${TITLE}

**Description:**
${DESCRIPTION}

## Documentation Requirements (MANDATORY)
After implementation, you MUST update project documentation:

1. **CHANGELOG or relevant docs**: If the project has a CHANGELOG.md, docs/ directory,
   or similar, add an entry describing what was implemented and any decisions made.

2. **Architecture/schema docs**: If you changed database schema, API contracts, or
   component architecture, update the corresponding docs (e.g., docs/database/schema/,
   API docs, component docs).

3. **CLAUDE.md**: If you discovered a new convention, gotcha, or hard-won rule during
   implementation, add it to the project's CLAUDE.md so future agents benefit.

4. **Implementation summary**: Create a file at \`.claude/agent-reports/${IDENTIFIER}.md\`
   with this structure:

   # ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}

   ## What was implemented
   [Brief summary of changes]

   ## Files changed
   [List of files and what changed in each]

   ## Decisions made
   [Any architectural or implementation decisions and why]

   ## Risks or follow-ups
   [Anything that needs human attention or future work]

   ## Tests
   [What was tested and coverage notes]

Commit all documentation updates as part of your work.

Do NOT modify files outside this worktree." --max-turns 500

        fi  # end scoped-worker vs standard agent
      fi

      # Save session ID for potential resume
      WORKTREE_ESCAPED=$(echo "$WORKTREE" | tr '/' '-' | sed 's/^-//')
      PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/${WORKTREE_ESCAPED}"
      if [ -d "$PROJECT_DIR" ]; then
        LATEST_SESSION=$(ls -t "$PROJECT_DIR"/*.jsonl 2>/dev/null | head -1 | xargs basename 2>/dev/null | sed 's/\.jsonl$//')
        if [ -n "$LATEST_SESSION" ]; then
          echo "$LATEST_SESSION" > "$SESSION_FILE"
        fi
      fi

      # --- Check if agent is blocked ---
      BLOCKED_FILE=".claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md"
      if [ -f "$BLOCKED_FILE" ]; then
        QUESTION=$(cat "$BLOCKED_FILE")
        cleanup_status_comments
        linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "🚧 **Agent blocked — needs human input**

${QUESTION}

---
*Reply to this comment, then re-add the \`${LABEL}\` label to resume the agent.*" >/dev/null 2>&1
        linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "Todo" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
        slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "🚧 [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] Agent blocked on ${IDENTIFIER} — needs human input"
        log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} blocked — question posted to Linear"
        stop_agent_db "$IDENTIFIER"
        exit 0
      fi

      # --- Check if agent made any commits ---
      COMMIT_COUNT=$(git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
      if [ "$COMMIT_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
        cleanup_status_comments
        linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "⚠️ **Agent made no changes** — the agent ran but did not commit any code. This may indicate the feature already exists, the task is unclear, or the agent couldn't proceed.

Check the agent log for details: \`$ISSUE_LOG\`" >/dev/null 2>&1
        linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "Todo" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
        log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} no commits — skipping CI/push"
        stop_agent_db "$IDENTIFIER"
        exit 0
      fi

      # --- Deterministic CI gate with retry loop ---
      CI_PASSED=false

      if [ -n "$CI_GATE" ] && [ -x "$CI_GATE" ]; then
        for ATTEMPT in $(seq 1 "$MAX_CI_RETRIES"); do
          post_status "🔍 **CI gate running** (attempt ${ATTEMPT}/${MAX_CI_RETRIES}) — type-check, tests, build"

          CI_OUTPUT_FILE="$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-ci-${ATTEMPT}.log"
          if "$CI_GATE" "$WORKTREE" > "$CI_OUTPUT_FILE" 2>&1; then
            CI_PASSED=true
            log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} CI gate passed (attempt ${ATTEMPT})"
            break
          fi

          log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} CI gate failed (attempt ${ATTEMPT}/${MAX_CI_RETRIES})"
          cat "$CI_OUTPUT_FILE" >> "$ISSUE_LOG"

          if [ "$ATTEMPT" -lt "$MAX_CI_RETRIES" ]; then
            CI_ERRORS=$(cat "$CI_OUTPUT_FILE")
            post_status "🔄 **CI gate failed (attempt ${ATTEMPT})** — spawning Claude to fix errors"

            claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --setting-sources project,local -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions.

## CI Gate Failed — Fix These Errors

The CI gate (type-check, tests, build) failed after your implementation of ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}.

Fix ALL failures below, even if they are unrelated to your changes. The CI gate must pass before we can push.

## CI Gate Output:
\`\`\`
${CI_ERRORS}
\`\`\`

## Rules:
- Fix every error shown above
- Run the failing commands yourself to verify fixes before committing
- Commit fixes with a message like 'fix: resolve CI failures for ${IDENTIFIER}'
- Do NOT push or open a PR
- Do NOT modify files outside this worktree" --max-turns 500 >> "$ISSUE_LOG" 2>&1
          else
            log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} CI gate FAILED after ${MAX_CI_RETRIES} attempts — not pushed"

            REPORT_FILE=".claude/agent-reports/${IDENTIFIER}.md"
            AGENT_REPORT=""
            if [ -f "$REPORT_FILE" ]; then
              AGENT_REPORT=$(cat "$REPORT_FILE")
            fi

            FAILURE_REPORT="# ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} — CI Failure Report

## Status: FAILED after ${MAX_CI_RETRIES} attempts — needs human intervention

## CI Gate Outputs
"
            for i in $(seq 1 "$MAX_CI_RETRIES"); do
              local ATTEMPT_LOG="$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-ci-${i}.log"
              if [ -f "$ATTEMPT_LOG" ]; then
                FAILURE_REPORT="${FAILURE_REPORT}
### Attempt ${i}
\`\`\`
$(tail -40 "$ATTEMPT_LOG")
\`\`\`
"
              fi
            done

            if [ -n "$AGENT_REPORT" ]; then
              FAILURE_REPORT="${FAILURE_REPORT}
## Agent's Fix Notes
${AGENT_REPORT}"
            fi

            FAILURE_REPORT="${FAILURE_REPORT}
## Files in worktree
\`\`\`
$(git diff --name-only origin/main 2>/dev/null || echo '(could not diff)')
\`\`\`

## Worktree location
\`${WORKTREE}\`
"

            cleanup_status_comments
            linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "❌ **CI gate failed after ${MAX_CI_RETRIES} attempts** — code not pushed.

${FAILURE_REPORT}" >/dev/null 2>&1
            slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "❌ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] CI failed after ${MAX_CI_RETRIES} attempts for ${IDENTIFIER} — needs human intervention"
          fi
        done

        if [ "$CI_PASSED" = true ]; then
          cleanup_status_comments

          if [ "$RESUME_REASON" != "feedback" ]; then
            REPORT_FILE=".claude/agent-reports/${IDENTIFIER}.md"
            if [ -f "$REPORT_FILE" ]; then
              REPORT_CONTENT=$(cat "$REPORT_FILE")
              linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "📋 **Implementation Report**

${REPORT_CONTENT}" >/dev/null 2>&1
            fi
          fi

          git push -u origin "$BRANCH" 2>&1

          EXISTING_PR=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json url --jq '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

          if [ -n "$EXISTING_PR" ]; then
            linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "In Review" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
            linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "✅ **Feedback addressed** — new commits pushed to existing PR: ${EXISTING_PR}

Ready for re-review." >/dev/null 2>&1
            slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "✅ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] ${IDENTIFIER} feedback addressed — PR updated: ${EXISTING_PR}"
            log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} feedback addressed — PR: $EXISTING_PR"

            # Auto-review existing PR if enabled
            if [ "$AUTO_REVIEW" = true ]; then
              local REVIEW_VERDICT
              REVIEW_VERDICT=$(auto_review_pr "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER")
              if [ "$AUTO_FIX" = true ] && [ "$REVIEW_VERDICT" = "request_changes" ]; then
                auto_fix_loop "$WORKTREE" "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER" "$REPO_NAME"
              fi
            fi
          else
            REPORT_FILE=".claude/agent-reports/${IDENTIFIER}.md"
            if [ -f "$REPORT_FILE" ]; then
              PR_BODY="$(cat "$REPORT_FILE")

---
*Implemented by autonomous Claude agent. CI gate passed: type-check, tests, build.*
Closes ${IDENTIFIER}"
            else
              PR_BODY="## ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}

${DESCRIPTION}

---
*Implemented by autonomous Claude agent. CI gate passed: type-check, tests, build.*
Closes ${IDENTIFIER}"
            fi

            PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
              --title "${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}" \
              --body "$PR_BODY" \
              --head "$BRANCH" \
              --base main 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -oE 'https://github\.com/[^ ]+')

            if [ -n "$PR_URL" ]; then
              linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "In Review" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
              linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "✅ **Agent completed** — CI gate passed, PR opened: ${PR_URL}

Ready for human review." >/dev/null 2>&1
              slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "✅ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] ${IDENTIFIER} completed — PR opened: ${PR_URL}"
              log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} done — PR: $PR_URL"

              # Auto-review new PR if enabled
              if [ "$AUTO_REVIEW" = true ]; then
                local REVIEW_VERDICT
                REVIEW_VERDICT=$(auto_review_pr "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER")
                if [ "$AUTO_FIX" = true ] && [ "$REVIEW_VERDICT" = "request_changes" ]; then
                  auto_fix_loop "$WORKTREE" "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER" "$REPO_NAME"
                fi
              fi
            else
              linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "⚠️ **Push succeeded but PR creation failed** — check logs: \`$ISSUE_LOG\`" >/dev/null 2>&1
              log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] ${IDENTIFIER} pushed but PR creation failed"
            fi
          fi
        fi
      else
        # No ci-gate — warn but still push
        cleanup_status_comments
        log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] WARNING: ci-gate not found, pushing without checks"
        git push -u origin "$BRANCH" 2>&1

        EXISTING_PR=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json url --jq '.url' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        if [ -n "$EXISTING_PR" ]; then
          linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "In Review" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
          linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "✅ **Feedback addressed (no CI gate)** — PR updated: ${EXISTING_PR}" >/dev/null 2>&1
          slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "✅ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] ${IDENTIFIER} feedback addressed — PR updated: ${EXISTING_PR}"
        else
          PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
            --title "${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}" \
            --body "${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} — implemented by autonomous Claude agent." \
            --head "$BRANCH" --base main 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -oE 'https://github\.com/[^ ]+')
          linear_set_state "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "In Review" "$TEAM_ID" >/dev/null 2>&1
          linear_comment "$API_KEY" "$ISSUE_ID" "⚠️ **Agent completed (no CI gate)** — PR: ${PR_URL}" >/dev/null 2>&1
          slack_notify "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" "✅ [${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}] ${IDENTIFIER} completed (no CI gate) — PR: ${PR_URL}"
        fi

        # Auto-review even without ci-gate
        if [ "$AUTO_REVIEW" = true ]; then
          local REVIEW_VERDICT
          REVIEW_VERDICT=$(auto_review_pr "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER")
          if [ "$AUTO_FIX" = true ] && [ "$REVIEW_VERDICT" = "request_changes" ]; then
            auto_fix_loop "$WORKTREE" "$REPO_DIR" "$BRANCH" "$IDENTIFIER" "$REPO_NAME"
          fi
        fi
      fi

      # Stop DB container
      stop_agent_db "$IDENTIFIER"

    ) >> "$ISSUE_LOG" 2>&1 &
    mkdir -p "$WORKTREE/.claude"
    echo $! > "$WORKTREE/.claude/agent.pid"

    log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Spawned agent for ${IDENTIFIER} (log: $ISSUE_LOG)"
  done

  rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
}

# --- Entry point ---

# Parse global flags first
while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --auto-review) AUTO_REVIEW=true; shift ;;
    --auto-fix) AUTO_FIX=true; AUTO_REVIEW=true; shift ;;
    --sequenced) SEQUENCED=true; shift ;;
    *) break ;;
  esac
done

case "${1:-}" in
  --all|"")
    WORKSPACES=$(jq -r '.workspaces | keys[]' "$CONFIG")
    while IFS= read -r WS; do
      API_KEY=$(resolve_api_key "$WS") || { log "No API key for workspace: $WS"; continue; }

      TEAMS=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WS\"].teams | to_entries[] | \"\(.key)|\(.value.repo)|\(.value.local_db // \"none\")\"" "$CONFIG")
      while IFS='|' read -r TEAM_NAME REPO_DIR LOCAL_DB; do
        run_agents_for_team "$API_KEY" "$WS" "$TEAM_NAME" "$REPO_DIR" "$LOCAL_DB"
      done <<< "$TEAMS"
    done <<< "$WORKSPACES"
    ;;
  --pipeline)
    shift
    PIPELINE_FILE="${1:-}"
    shift || true
    PIPELINE_WS=""
    PIPELINE_TEAM=""
    PIPELINE_DRY_RUN=false
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        --workspace) PIPELINE_WS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --team) PIPELINE_TEAM="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --dry-run) PIPELINE_DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
        *) shift ;;
      esac
    done
    if [ -z "$PIPELINE_FILE" ]; then
      echo "Usage: claude-agent-runner --pipeline <requirements.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--dry-run]"
      exit 1
    fi
    run_pipeline "$PIPELINE_FILE" "$PIPELINE_WS" "$PIPELINE_TEAM" "$PIPELINE_DRY_RUN"
    ;;
  --spec)
    shift
    SPEC_FILE="${1:-}"
    shift || true
    SPEC_WS=""
    SPEC_TEAM=""
    SPEC_INTERACTIVE=false
    SPEC_CREATE=false
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        --workspace) SPEC_WS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --team) SPEC_TEAM="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --interactive) SPEC_INTERACTIVE=true; shift ;;
        --create) SPEC_CREATE=true; shift ;;
        *) shift ;;
      esac
    done
    if [ -z "$SPEC_FILE" ]; then
      echo "Usage: claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--interactive] [--create]"
      exit 1
    fi
    run_spec "$SPEC_FILE" "$SPEC_WS" "$SPEC_TEAM" "$SPEC_INTERACTIVE" "$SPEC_CREATE"
    ;;
  --review-pr)
    shift
    REVIEW_PR_NUM="${1:-}"
    shift || true
    REVIEW_REPO=""
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        --repo) REVIEW_REPO="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *) shift ;;
      esac
    done
    if [ -z "$REVIEW_PR_NUM" ] || [ -z "$REVIEW_REPO" ]; then
      echo "Usage: claude-agent-runner --review-pr <number> --repo <path>"
      exit 1
    fi
    cd "$REVIEW_REPO" || { echo "ERROR: Repo not found: $REVIEW_REPO"; exit 1; }
    BRANCH=$(gh pr view "$REVIEW_PR_NUM" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName' 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -z "$BRANCH" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: PR #${REVIEW_PR_NUM} not found"
      exit 1
    fi
    auto_review_pr "$REVIEW_REPO" "$BRANCH" "PR-${REVIEW_PR_NUM}"
    ;;
  --cleanup)
    jq -r '.workspaces | to_entries[] | .value.teams | to_entries[] | .value.repo' "$CONFIG" | while IFS= read -r repo; do
      cleanup_worktrees "$repo"
    done
    ;;
  --status)
    show_status
    ;;
  --help|-h)
    echo "claude-agent-runner — Autonomous Claude agents from Linear issues"
    echo ""
    echo "Usage:"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner                                   Poll all workspaces (default)"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --auto-review                     Poll + auto-review PRs"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --auto-fix                        Poll + auto-review + fix loop"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --sequenced                       Only pick up unblocked issues"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --pipeline <file.md> [options]    Parse requirements → Linear"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md> [options]        Generate spec from idea"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --review-pr <num> --repo <path>   Standalone PR review"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --cleanup                         Clean up merged worktrees"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --status                          Show active agents"
    echo "  claude-agent-runner --help                            Show this help"
    echo ""
    echo "Pipeline options:"
    echo "  --workspace <name>   Target workspace (default: first in config)"
    echo "  --team <name>        Target team (default: first in workspace)"
    echo "  --dry-run            Print parsed stories without creating issues"
    echo ""
    echo "Spec options:"
    echo "  --workspace <name>   Target workspace (default: first in config)"
    echo "  --team <name>        Target team (default: first in workspace)"
    echo "  --interactive        Interactive mode — Claude asks clarifying questions"
    echo "  --create             Chain output to --pipeline to create Linear issues"
    echo ""
    echo "Global flags (combinable with any mode):"
    echo "  --auto-review        Auto-review PRs after creation/update"
    echo "  --auto-fix           Auto-review + fix loop (up to $MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS iterations)"
    echo "  --sequenced          Skip issues with incomplete blockers"
    echo ""
    echo "Config:  $CONFIG"
    echo "Logs:    $LOG_DIR"
    echo ""
    echo "API Keys (set per workspace via env vars):"
    echo "  Option 1: Set 'api_key_env' in config → references a custom env var name"
    echo "  Option 2: Export LINEAR_API_KEY_<WORKSPACE> (auto-detected)"
    echo "  Option 3: Export LINEAR_API_KEY (global fallback)"
    echo ""
    echo "How it works:"
    echo "  1. Label a Linear issue with '$LABEL'"
    echo "  2. Runner picks it up, moves to In Progress, spawns Claude"
    echo "  3. Claude implements in an isolated worktree"
    echo "  4. Runner runs CI gate, pushes, creates PR"
    echo "  5. Optional: auto-review and auto-fix cycle"
    echo "  6. You review the PR"
    echo ""
    echo "Orchestration (multi-agent):"
    echo "  Add the '${ORCH_LABEL}' label to an issue to decompose into subtasks."
    echo "  Enable in config: orchestrator.enabled = true"
    echo "  The orchestrator decomposes, creates sub-issues, supervises workers,"
    echo "  validates results, and creates a combined PR."
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Unknown option: $1 (try --help)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

wait
log "All agents finished."

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# claude-agent-runner — Polls Linear for issues labeled "agent", spawns isolated Claude instances
#
# Usage:
#   claude-agent-runner                           Run for all configured workspaces/teams
#   claude-agent-runner --auto-review             Run + auto-review PRs after creation
#   claude-agent-runner --auto-fix                Run + auto-review + auto-fix loop
#   claude-agent-runner --sequenced               Only pick up issues whose blockers are done
#   claude-agent-runner --pipeline <file.md>      Parse requirements into Linear issues
#   claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md>          Generate spec from idea, optionally create issues
#   claude-agent-runner --review-pr <num> --repo <path>  Standalone PR review
#   claude-agent-runner --cleanup                 Clean up merged worktrees
#   claude-agent-runner --status                  Show active agents and worktrees
#   claude-agent-runner --help                    Show usage
#
# Config: ~/.config/claude-agents/config.json
# Logs:   ~/.config/claude-agents/logs/
#
# Requires: jq, claude, git, gh, curl
# Optional: docker (for local_db), pnpm

set -uo pipefail

CONFIG="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/config.json"
LOG_DIR="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/logs"
LOCK_DIR="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/locks"
SECRETS="$HOME/.config/claude-agents/secrets.env"

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$LOCK_DIR"

# Source secrets (API keys) — works in cron/background where ~/.zshrc isn't loaded
if [ -f "$SECRETS" ]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1090
  source "$SECRETS"
fi

# Load config
if [ ! -f "$CONFIG" ]; then
  echo "Config not found: $CONFIG"
  exit 1
fi

LABEL=$(jq -r '.label // "agent"' "$CONFIG")
MAX_PARALLEL=$(jq -r '.max_parallel // 3' "$CONFIG")
MAX_TURNS=$(jq -r '.max_turns // 15' "$CONFIG")
MAX_CI_RETRIES=$(jq -r '.max_ci_retries // 3' "$CONFIG")
MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS=$(jq -r '.max_review_fix_iterations // 3' "$CONFIG")

# Orchestrator config
ORCH_ENABLED=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.enabled // false' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_LABEL=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.label // "Orchestrate"' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_AUTO=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.auto // false' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_MAX_SUBTASKS=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.max_subtasks // 8' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_MAX_TURNS=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.max_orchestrator_turns // 50' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_POLL_INTERVAL=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.poll_interval_seconds // 30' "$CONFIG")
ORCH_MAX_RETRIES=$(jq -r '.orchestrator.max_validation_retries // 2' "$CONFIG")

# Global flags (set by CLI parsing)
AUTO_REVIEW=false
AUTO_FIX=false
SEQUENCED=false

log() {
  echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1"
}

# --- Resolve ci-gate path ---

resolve_ci_gate() {
  # 1. Explicit config path
  local CONFIG_PATH
  CONFIG_PATH=$(jq -r '.ci_gate_path // empty' "$CONFIG")
  if [ -n "$CONFIG_PATH" ] && [ -x "$CONFIG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "$CONFIG_PATH"
    return 0
  fi

  # 2. Sibling to this script (both in ~/.local/bin/ or same dir)
  local SCRIPT_DIR
  SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
  if [ -x "$SCRIPT_DIR/ci-gate" ]; then
    echo "$SCRIPT_DIR/ci-gate"
    return 0
  fi

  # 3. PATH lookup
  local PATH_CI
  PATH_CI=$(command -v ci-gate 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$PATH_CI" ] && [ -x "$PATH_CI" ]; then
    echo "$PATH_CI"
    return 0
  fi

  echo ""
}

# --- Linear API helpers (all take API_KEY as first arg) ---

linear_graphql() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local QUERY="$2"
  curl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: $API_KEY" \
    -d "{\"query\": $(echo "$QUERY" | jq -Rs .)}"
}

linear_comment() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local BODY="$3"
  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { commentCreate(input: { issueId: \"$ISSUE_ID\", body: $(echo "$BODY" | jq -Rs .) }) { success comment { id } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.commentCreate.comment.id // empty'
}

linear_delete_comment() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local COMMENT_ID="$2"
  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { commentDelete(id: \"$COMMENT_ID\") { success } }" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

linear_set_state() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local STATE_NAME="$3"
  local TEAM_ID="$4"

  local STATE_ID
  STATE_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ workflowStates(filter: { name: { eq: \"$STATE_NAME\" }, team: { id: { eq: \"$TEAM_ID\" } } }) { nodes { id name } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.workflowStates.nodes[0].id // empty')

  if [ -n "$STATE_ID" ]; then
    linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { issueUpdate(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\", input: { stateId: \"$STATE_ID\" }) { success } }"
  fi
}

linear_get_comments() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { comments { nodes { body createdAt user { name } } } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issue.comments.nodes | sort_by(.createdAt) | .[] | "[\(.user.name // "system")] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null
}

linear_remove_label() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local LABEL_ID="$3"

  local CURRENT_LABELS
  CURRENT_LABELS=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { labels { nodes { id } } } }" \
    | jq -r "[.data.issue.labels.nodes[].id | select(. != \"$LABEL_ID\")] | join(\",\")")

  local LABEL_ARRAY
  if [ -z "$CURRENT_LABELS" ]; then
    LABEL_ARRAY="[]"
  else
    LABEL_ARRAY=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABELS" | tr ',' '\n' | jq -R . | jq -s .)
  fi

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { issueUpdate(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\", input: { labelIds: $LABEL_ARRAY }) { success } }"
}

linear_add_label() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"
  local LABEL_ID="$3"

  local CURRENT_LABELS
  CURRENT_LABELS=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { labels { nodes { id } } } }" \
    | jq -r "[.data.issue.labels.nodes[].id] + [\"$LABEL_ID\"] | unique | join(\",\")")

  local LABEL_ARRAY
  LABEL_ARRAY=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABELS" | tr ',' '\n' | jq -R . | jq -s .)

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation { issueUpdate(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\", input: { labelIds: $LABEL_ARRAY }) { success } }"
}

linear_create_sub_issue() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local TEAM_ID="$2"
  local PARENT_ID="$3"
  local TITLE="$4"
  local DESCRIPTION="$5"
  local LABEL_IDS="$6"

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
    issueCreate(input: {
      teamId: \"$TEAM_ID\"
      parentId: \"$PARENT_ID\"
      title: $(echo "$TITLE" | jq -Rs .)
      description: $(echo "$DESCRIPTION" | jq -Rs .)
      labelIds: $LABEL_IDS
    }) {
      success
      issue { id identifier }
    }
  }"
}

linear_get_issue_state() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local ISSUE_ID="$2"

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$ISSUE_ID\") { state { name type } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issue.state.type // "unknown"'
}

linear_get_sub_issues() {
  local API_KEY="$1"
  local PARENT_ID="$2"

  linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issue(id: \"$PARENT_ID\") { children { nodes { id identifier title state { name type } } } } }" \
    | jq -r '.data.issue.children.nodes // []'
}

# --- GitHub PR feedback helpers ---

gather_pr_feedback() {
  local REPO_DIR="$1"
  local BRANCH="$2"
  local FEEDBACK=""

  cd "$REPO_DIR" || return 0

  # Check if a PR exists for this branch
  local PR_NUMBER
  PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json number --jq '.number' 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] && return 0

  local OWNER_REPO
  OWNER_REPO=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner --jq '.nameWithOwner' 2>/dev/null) || return 0

  # Top-level PR comments
  local PR_COMMENTS
  PR_COMMENTS=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json comments --jq '.comments[] | "[\(.author.login)] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$PR_COMMENTS" ]; then
    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## PR Comments
${PR_COMMENTS}

"
  fi

  # Review summaries
  local REVIEWS
  REVIEWS=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews --jq '.reviews[] | select(.body != "") | "[\(.author.login) - \(.state)] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$REVIEWS" ]; then
    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## Review Summaries
${REVIEWS}

"
  fi

  # Inline file-level comments (review comments on specific lines)
  local INLINE
  INLINE=$(gh api "repos/${OWNER_REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments" --jq '.[] | "[\(.user.login)] \(.path):\(.line // .original_line) — \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$INLINE" ]; then
    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## Inline Review Comments
${INLINE}

"
  fi

  echo "$FEEDBACK"
}

# --- Slack notification helper ---

slack_notify() {
  local WEBHOOK_URL="$1"
  local MESSAGE="$2"
  [ -z "$WEBHOOK_URL" ] && return 0
  curl -s -X POST "$WEBHOOK_URL" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d "{\"text\": $(echo "$MESSAGE" | jq -Rs .)}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}

# --- Resolve API key for a workspace ---

resolve_api_key() {
  local WORKSPACE="$1"

  # Check for env var name in config
  local ENV_VAR
  ENV_VAR=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].api_key_env // empty" "$CONFIG")
  if [ -n "$ENV_VAR" ]; then
    local KEY="${!ENV_VAR:-}"
    if [ -n "$KEY" ]; then
      echo "$KEY"
      return 0
    fi
    echo "WARNING: env var $ENV_VAR not set for workspace $WORKSPACE" >&2
  fi

  # Fallback: LINEAR_API_KEY_<WORKSPACE>
  local AUTO_VAR="LINEAR_API_KEY_$(echo "$WORKSPACE" | tr '[:lower:]-' '[:upper:]_')"
  local KEY="${!AUTO_VAR:-}"
  if [ -n "$KEY" ]; then
    echo "$KEY"
    return 0
  fi

  # Last fallback: global LINEAR_API_KEY
  if [ -n "${LINEAR_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
    echo "$LINEAR_API_KEY"
    return 0
  fi

  echo "ERROR: No API key found for workspace $WORKSPACE" >&2
  echo "  Set one of: $ENV_VAR, $AUTO_VAR, or LINEAR_API_KEY" >&2
  return 1
}

# --- Resolve Slack webhook for a workspace ---

resolve_slack_webhook() {
  local WORKSPACE="$1"

  local ENV_VAR
  ENV_VAR=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].slack_webhook_env // empty" "$CONFIG")
  if [ -n "$ENV_VAR" ]; then
    local URL="${!ENV_VAR:-}"
    if [ -n "$URL" ]; then
      echo "$URL"
      return 0
    fi
  fi

  local AUTO_VAR="SLACK_WEBHOOK_$(echo "$WORKSPACE" | tr '[:lower:]-' '[:upper:]_')"
  local URL="${!AUTO_VAR:-}"
  if [ -n "$URL" ]; then
    echo "$URL"
    return 0
  fi

  echo ""
}

# --- Docker-per-agent DB isolation ---

start_agent_db() {
  local WORKTREE="$1"
  local IDENTIFIER="$2"
  local REPO_DIR="$3"
  local DB_TYPE="${4:-none}"

  [ "$DB_TYPE" = "none" ] && return 0

  local REPO_NAME
  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
  local CONTAINER="agent-db-${IDENTIFIER}"

  # On resume: check if container is already running
  if docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx "$CONTAINER"; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] DB container $CONTAINER already running, reusing"
    local EXISTING_PORT
    EXISTING_PORT=$(docker port "$CONTAINER" 5432 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d: -f2)
    if [ -n "$EXISTING_PORT" ]; then
      _write_db_env "$WORKTREE" "$EXISTING_PORT" "$DB_TYPE"
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  # Remove stopped container with same name if it exists
  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

  log "[$REPO_NAME] Starting DB container for ${IDENTIFIER} (type: ${DB_TYPE})"

  # Start postgres container with random ephemeral port
  docker run -d \
    --name "$CONTAINER" \
    --label "claude-agent=true" \
    -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
    -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
    -e POSTGRES_DB=agent \
    -p 0:5432 \
    postgres:16-alpine >/dev/null 2>&1

  if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Failed to start DB container for ${IDENTIFIER}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Read assigned port
  local DB_PORT
  DB_PORT=$(docker port "$CONTAINER" 5432 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d: -f2)
  if [ -z "$DB_PORT" ]; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Could not determine DB port for ${IDENTIFIER}"
    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for pg_isready (max 30s)
  local WAITED=0
  while [ $WAITED -lt 30 ]; do
    if docker exec "$CONTAINER" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
    WAITED=$((WAITED + 1))
  done

  if [ $WAITED -ge 30 ]; then
    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: DB container for ${IDENTIFIER} not ready after 30s"
    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1
    return 1
  fi

  # Run migrations if present
  local MIGRATION_DIR=""
